title: The 20% Down Myth Keeping Renters Trapped 14 Years
hook: Seventy-three percent of renters think they need twenty percent down to buy — and that single belief is costing them fourteen extra years of wealth-building.
angle: The 20% rule was created by banks in 1934 to protect lenders, not buyers. FHA loans need 3.5%, VA and USDA need 0%. The myth costs more than PMI ever did.
audience: Renters in their 20s-40s who believe homeownership is out of reach due to down payment requirements
key_numbers: 73% of renters believe they need 20% down (survey data)
14 extra years of renting = $252,000 in rent with $0 equity
